AI/ML.text:
Perplexity's computer-use product is built around GLM 5.2
https://www.cnbc.com/2026/07/10/the-ai-race-is-shifting-from-bigger-models-to-cheaper-smarter-systems.html

tweet.text:
The mega bull case for AI infrastructure would be *if* market share shifted away from certain frontier labs with 90%+ inference margins toward cheaper models, whether open-source or closed.

It would increase the ROI on AI spend for end customers by increasing intelligence per dollar, which would drive incremental token demand. Margin dollars would effectively get redistributed from the frontier labs to AI infrastructure providers. The infra winners would be those with the lowest per token cost and the winners at the model layer would be those with the highest token efficiency.

There are many reasons Jensen is so focused on open source, but this is likely the most important one as I think he is probably less worried about a monopsony these days. Lower margin % at the model layer = more margin $ at the infra layer all else equal.

With SpaceX and Meta being vertically integrated and possessing the #3 and #4 models respectively it is more possible than ever. Note that Grok 4.5 is ahead

AI/ML.text:
The Decade Ahead
> The next decade will be shaped by three structural forces: AI-driven automation, a shift to a multipolar world, and demographic change, with intelligence becoming the world's most valuable resource.
> With agents, robotics, and edge AI driving a massive productivity boom.
Source:
https://members.delphidigital.io/reports/the-decade-ahead?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social#automation-multi-polarity-demographics-d836

AI and measurement are becoming the default marketing priorities
The chart from IAB’s 2026 Outlook Study shows where marketer focus increased YoY in 2026 vs 2025.
The biggest move is generative AI use in media campaigns:
78%
in 2026 vs
62%
in 2025. A new focus area also appears around optimizing content for AI-generated answers at
73%
, while cross-platform measurement rises to
72%
from
64%
.
That reads like a market that wants less guesswork and more proof. When budgets get tighter, visibility only matters if it can be found, measured, and repeated – which is the same pressure shaping Web3 marketing next.
Data source:
IAB’s 2026 Outlook Study
Related read:
Web3 Marketing in 2027: Adapting to bear market budgets

AI/ML.text:
The Reverse Information Paradox
> In Arrow’s “Information Paradox,” the seller risks giving away knowledge in order to sell it.
> AI creates the Reverse Information Paradox
> In the AI age, the buyer risks giving away knowledge, just in order to use what they bought. You pay for intelligence twice, once with money, and again with knowledge.
> Seller learns more about you with every interaction, while you learn little about what it learns in return.
> Enterprises need a hard trust boundary around their data, evals, and learning loops.
> A company should be able to use a model without giving up the knowledge that makes it unique.
Source:
https://x.com/satyanadella/status/2076323181154230284

The Decade Ahead
3 structural macro forces —> AI and automation, multi-polarity, and shifting demographics/longevity are permanently reshaping the global economy, making conviction-driven investment in capital and technology the primary pathway for future value creation and wealth preservation
https://members.delphidigital.io/reports/the-decade-ahead

An Investor’s Handbook I - AI Optics
Driven by massive AI computing demands, optical connections are progressively replacing copper from long-distance links down to chip-level architectures because photons offer vastly superior bandwidth with lower energy loss and heat
While copper still holds a cost and latency edge for short-range connections inside the server rack, the broader optical transition hinges on integrating mature silicon chips with specialized indium phosphide light sources and advanced packaging
https://nuttycld.substack.com/p/an-investors-handbook-i-ai-optics

Crypto Infrastructure.text:
SEC proposes "Regulation Crypto" framework for easier capital raising –
Link
The SEC is preparing a new crypto regulation proposal as early as July 2026. It would allow startups to raise up to $5 million with simple white-paper disclosures and projects up to $75 million in any 12-month period.
Matters because:
It would mark a shift from enforcement-heavy regulation toward a more structured capital-raising path for crypto startups.

tweet.text:
SEC is preparing to propose a new crypto regulation as early as July 2026 that would make it significantly easier for startups to raise capital. 

Highlighted in a recent Yahoo Finance segment with analyst Scott Melker, the plan—referred to as Regulation Crypto—creates temporary exemptions from full securities registration under the 1933 Securities Act. 

It includes a startup pathway allowing projects to operate for up to four years with simple white-paper disclosures and raise up to $5 million, a fundraising exemption permitting up to $75 million in any 12-month period with basic disclosures, and a safe harbor for decentralizing projects that covers DeFi and tokenized securities. 

The goal, according to SEC Chair Paul Atkins, is to provide regulatory clarity, reduce enforcement risks, attract activity onshore, and position the United States as the global leader in crypto while still protecting investors.

This marks a clear shift from the prior enforcement-focused approach to one th

tweet.text:
#PeckShieldAlert  Specter has reported that @hedera Network appeared to be exploited, with $5.25M in stolen funds already bridged from #Hedera Mainnet to Ethereum.

The hacker originally funded 1 ETH from #TornadoCash and now holds 2.36K ETH ($4.25M) & 15.58 WBTC ($1M) in the wallet.

Crypto Infrastructure.text:
Bitcoin ETF inflows resume; July 10 saw $90.4M net inflow –
Link
After Thursday's $95.3 million outflow, Bitcoin spot ETFs posted a $90.4 million net inflow on July 10. BlackRock's IBIT led with $86.8 million, while Ethereum ETFs saw $18.4 million of inflows.
Matters because:
The reversal suggests renewed institutional demand and may help support price stability above $63,000.

Daily news snapshot: Altcoins watchlist
1. Arbitrum (ARB): +20%
• Market cap: ≈$3.5 billion
• ARB surged about 20% on July 10 after Robinhood Chain launched using Arbitrum as its underlying technology provider.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain will allocate 10% of its protocol net revenue back to the Arbitrum ecosystem.
• The move highlights ecosystem expansion and revenue-sharing mechanics.
• Current activity remains primarily meme-coin driven.
Matters because:
The Robinhood Chain tie-in gives Arbitrum a clear ecosystem catalyst, though the current usage mix is still largely speculative.
2. Cash Cat (CASHCAT): +40% intraday
• Market cap: $180 million
• The meme coin briefly hit an all-time high market cap of $180 million.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain's explosive first week with $1 billion+ in DEX volume.
Matters because:
It shows how quickly speculative meme-coin activity can accelerate on a new chain, but the move lacks fundamental backing.
3. Solana (SOL): ETF inflow of $150,500
• Market cap: ≈$70 billion
• Solana spot ETF (TSOL) recorded a net inflow of $150,500 on July 10, with total SOL spot ETF assets at $901 million.
Matters because:
Even a modest inflow points to continued institutional interest in Solana despite broader caution.

Swift launches blockchain ledger with 17 major banks –
Link
Swift launched a blockchain-based shared ledger with 17 banks, including Citi, HSBC, UBS, BNY, Standard Chartered, and Wells Fargo. The system enables 24/7 tokenized fund transfers while maintaining regulatory compliance.
Matters because:
It signals growing institutional adoption of blockchain infrastructure and gives Swift a direct answer to stablecoins and programmable payments.

Circle receives federal banking charter approval –
Link
Circle received final approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a federally regulated national trust bank. The approval lets the USDC issuer custody its own reserves and hold digital assets for institutional clients.
Matters because:
It brings stablecoins closer to traditional financial infrastructure and could accelerate institutional adoption of tokenized assets.

DeFi.text:
Brave releases BAT roadmap 4.0 with unified wallet and stablecoin payment protocol –
Link
Brave launched BAT roadmap 4.0 on July 9, adding a unified Brave Wallet and a new stablecoin payment protocol called BravePay.
• Unified wallet with traditional payments + self-custodied crypto
• BravePay, a privacy-focused stablecoin payment protocol
• Planned Brave Rewards payment cards
• Creator contribution protocol for AI content usage
Matters because:
The roadmap expands BAT’s ecosystem beyond basic rewards and could support wider adoption.

Ryan Watkins: Crypto Is Entering Its Biggest Adoption Phase
> Ryan Watkins argues blockchains are becoming the global API for money and finance, set to power stablecoins, tokenized equities, perps, prediction markets, onchain credit, and more.
> Bringing crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents, with effort to come from both crypto-native apps (e.g Phantom and Fomo) and incumbents like Robinhood and Stripe.
> “Time to look past any month-to-month volatility and focus on the bigger picture unfolding over coming quarters and years…Time to lock in.”
Source:
https://x.com/RyanWatkins_/status/2075595100789318068

tweet.text:
Blockchains are a global API for money and finance.

We’re going to plug this API into every app where it makes sense over the coming years and bring crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents.

Stablecoin payments + savings, tokenized assets, onchain credit markets, perps for everything, prediction markets… the list goes on.

All these products are composable, so builders will only be limited by their imaginations on what this could all look like.

Some efforts will be homegrown like Phantom and Fomo.

Others will be established players like Robinhood and Stripe plugging it in and championing this technology.

In any case we will win.

DeFi.text:
Gauntlet raises $125 million from SBI Holdings –
Link
DeFi risk management platform Gauntlet secured $125M in exclusive financing from SBI Holdings. The deal came in above its 2022 Series B valuation of roughly $1B.
Matters because:
It shows continued institutional demand for DeFi risk infrastructure and vault curation.

An Investor's Handbook II - AI Power (1)
AI datacenter expansion faces a major timeline mismatch between rapid GPU releases (~18 months) and lengthy power grid interconnection approvals (~61 months)
As a result, the market value has shifted from electricity generation capacity to companies that can optimize energy efficiency, reduce loss, or accelerate delivery timelines across the power path
https://nuttycld.substack.com/p/an-investors-handbook-ii-ai-power

Sky Protocol hits record $419M annualized gross revenue –
Link
Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million in June 2026. Cumulative yield to sUSDS holders exceeded $250 million, and Sky Reserves grew to $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March.
Matters because:
The numbers point to strong DeFi fundamentals and sustainable yield generation.

tweet.text:
Sky Protocol's Annualized Gross Revenue Run Rate Hits Record $419M

Sky Frontier Foundation said in its June 2026 financial and operational update that Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million, based on the last three Monthly Settlement Cycles. Cumulative yield accrued to sUSDS holders through the Sky Savings Rate has surpassed $250 million since inception, while Sky Reserves closed June at $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March. Grove, one of Sky's Prime Agents, launched its GROVE governance token in June. Separately, a fixed-yield product for sUSDS introduced through http://Sky.Money and Pendle Finance reached $44.1 million in TVL during its first month.

tweet.text:
Morgan Stanley: Humanoids

Shifts in BoM Cost Composition (2026e–2040e)

> Rising Chips/Compute Cost: The share of BoM cost for Chips is projected to triple, rising sharply from 5% in 2026e to 15% by 2040e.

> Sensors (Force/Tactile): Remain the largest single component cost initially at 24% in 2026e, but steadily decline to 17% by 2040e.

> Motors: Hold a relatively stable but slightly declining share, moving from 22% down to 19%.

> Reducers: Drop slightly from 15% to 12%.

Projected Demand & Market Growth

> Motors: Projected to reach a massive US$2,073bn in market size, representing a 170x increase.

> Reducers: Expected to reach US$1,370bn (a 157x increase).

> Edge Compute: Reaches US$1,233bn, driven by the single highest multiplier growth of 1,904x.

> Bearings: Set for a staggering 370x growth to a value of US$316bn.

> Cameras & LiDAR: Cameras scale by 84x (US$268bn), while LiDAR scales by 40x (US$128bn).

The mega bull case for AI infrastructure would be *if* market share shifted away from certain frontier labs with 90%+ inference margins toward cheaper models, whether open-source or closed.

It would increase the ROI on AI spend for end customers by increasing intelligence per dollar, which would drive incremental token demand. Margin dollars would effectively get redistributed from the frontier labs to AI infrastructure providers. The infra winners would be those with the lowest per token cost and the winners at the model layer would be those with the highest token efficiency.

There are many reasons Jensen is so focused on open source, but this is likely the most important one as I think he is probably less worried about a monopsony these days. Lower margin % at the model layer = more margin $ at the infra layer all else equal.

With SpaceX and Meta being vertically integrated and possessing the #3 and #4 models respectively it is more possible than ever. Note that Grok 4.5 is ahead

Prediction Markets.text:
overview of the thesis and investments made by theory ventures
https://x.com/ttunguz/status/2075629199252144550

tweet.text:
Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir

tweet.text:
Announcing ComputeConnect, the financial industry’s first exchange-for-physical (EFP) network for compute, coming soon from Architect and @ComputeDesk. ComputeConnect links US exchange-traded compute futures to compute capacity delivery.

Exchange-listed cash-settled compute futures are entering US markets to correct course on the current AI economy, reorienting debt to long-term growth:

 • Creating price discovery and transparency independent of any single capacity provider.
 • Establishing a forward curve for measuring deprecation and forecasting supply and demand.
 • Providing financial hedges for compute consumers and producers.
 • Enabling hedge funds, ETF companies, and traders to gain long and short financial exposure to compute.

US cash-settled compute futures lack a physical delivery mechanism, and ComputeConnect fills this gap.

Existing physically settled futures such as energy and agriculturals require their clearing house (DCO) to set a uniform standard for the grade and

RWA/Tokenization.text:
Swift launches blockchain ledger with 17 major banks –
Link
Swift launched a blockchain-based shared ledger with 17 banks, including Citi, HSBC, UBS, BNY, Standard Chartered, and Wells Fargo. The system enables 24/7 tokenized fund transfers while maintaining regulatory compliance.
Matters because:
It signals growing institutional adoption of blockchain infrastructure and gives Swift a direct answer to stablecoins and programmable payments.

Circle receives federal banking charter approval –
Link
Circle received final approval from the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency to establish a federally regulated national trust bank. The approval lets the USDC issuer custody its own reserves and hold digital assets for institutional clients.
Matters because:
It brings stablecoins closer to traditional financial infrastructure and could accelerate institutional adoption of tokenized assets.

SEC proposes "Regulation Crypto" framework for easier capital raising –
Link
The SEC is preparing a new crypto regulation proposal as early as July 2026. It would allow startups to raise up to $5 million with simple white-paper disclosures and projects up to $75 million in any 12-month period.
Matters because:
It would mark a shift from enforcement-heavy regulation toward a more structured capital-raising path for crypto startups.

tweet.text:
SEC is preparing to propose a new crypto regulation as early as July 2026 that would make it significantly easier for startups to raise capital. 

Highlighted in a recent Yahoo Finance segment with analyst Scott Melker, the plan—referred to as Regulation Crypto—creates temporary exemptions from full securities registration under the 1933 Securities Act. 

It includes a startup pathway allowing projects to operate for up to four years with simple white-paper disclosures and raise up to $5 million, a fundraising exemption permitting up to $75 million in any 12-month period with basic disclosures, and a safe harbor for decentralizing projects that covers DeFi and tokenized securities. 

The goal, according to SEC Chair Paul Atkins, is to provide regulatory clarity, reduce enforcement risks, attract activity onshore, and position the United States as the global leader in crypto while still protecting investors.

This marks a clear shift from the prior enforcement-focused approach to one th

RWA/Tokenization.text:
Ryan Watkins: Crypto Is Entering Its Biggest Adoption Phase
> Ryan Watkins argues blockchains are becoming the global API for money and finance, set to power stablecoins, tokenized equities, perps, prediction markets, onchain credit, and more.
> Bringing crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents, with effort to come from both crypto-native apps (e.g Phantom and Fomo) and incumbents like Robinhood and Stripe.
> “Time to look past any month-to-month volatility and focus on the bigger picture unfolding over coming quarters and years…Time to lock in.”
Source:
https://x.com/RyanWatkins_/status/2075595100789318068

tweet.text:
Blockchains are a global API for money and finance.

We’re going to plug this API into every app where it makes sense over the coming years and bring crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents.

Stablecoin payments + savings, tokenized assets, onchain credit markets, perps for everything, prediction markets… the list goes on.

All these products are composable, so builders will only be limited by their imaginations on what this could all look like.

Some efforts will be homegrown like Phantom and Fomo.

Others will be established players like Robinhood and Stripe plugging it in and championing this technology.

In any case we will win.

RWA/Tokenization.text:
New exchange to farm on Robinhood chain
• Robinhood-backed DEX for tokenized equities + perps
• Unified account, dual product: 24/7 Stock Tokens (spot) + cross-margined perps up to 50x
• Non-custodial with proof-of-reserves
Link:
https://waitlist.arcus.xyz/s/ABA

EU expands MiCA regulatory scope to cover tokenization and non-EU stablecoin issuers –
Link
The European Commission is seeking feedback, with a September 30 deadline, on expanding MiCA to include tokenized assets and non-EU stablecoin issuers. MiCA's grandfathering period ended July 1.
Matters because:
The move signals broader EU crypto oversight and likely higher compliance costs for platforms.

Reserve launches five AI supply chain token funds on BNB Chain –
Link
Reserve Protocol launched five decentralized token funds tracking the AI infrastructure supply chain on BNB Chain.
• $BUILDOUT — overall AI stack
• $POWER — energy
• $PHOTON — photonics
• $NEOCLOUD — compute capacity
• $ROBOTS — robotics
Each fund is built from tokenized US-listed companies and trades 24/7.
Matters because:
It expands on-chain access to AI infrastructure exposure through tokenized products.

Culturally Crypto, Practically TradFi
> Robinhood is using crypto to vertically integrate brokerage, tokenized stocks, perps, lending, and settlement through its own chain, leveraging its 28M users who are culturally aligned with crypto but remain in TradFi.
> The chain combines RWAs for utility and now memes for engagement, with a future stablecoin potentially becoming the final layer, capturing float revenue, improving margins, and giving Robinhood control over the full financial stack.
Source:
https://x.com/Decentralisedco/status/2075587828176867709

Raises/Funding.text:
Gauntlet raises $125 million from SBI Holdings –
Link
DeFi risk management platform Gauntlet secured $125M in exclusive financing from SBI Holdings. The deal came in above its 2022 Series B valuation of roughly $1B.
Matters because:
It shows continued institutional demand for DeFi risk infrastructure and vault curation.

Sky Protocol hits record $419M annualized gross revenue –
Link
Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million in June 2026. Cumulative yield to sUSDS holders exceeded $250 million, and Sky Reserves grew to $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March.
Matters because:
The numbers point to strong DeFi fundamentals and sustainable yield generation.

tweet.text:
Sky Protocol's Annualized Gross Revenue Run Rate Hits Record $419M

Sky Frontier Foundation said in its June 2026 financial and operational update that Sky Protocol's annualized gross revenue run rate reached a record $419.08 million, based on the last three Monthly Settlement Cycles. Cumulative yield accrued to sUSDS holders through the Sky Savings Rate has surpassed $250 million since inception, while Sky Reserves closed June at $82.5 million, up $33.7 million since March. Grove, one of Sky's Prime Agents, launched its GROVE governance token in June. Separately, a fixed-yield product for sUSDS introduced through http://Sky.Money and Pendle Finance reached $44.1 million in TVL during its first month.

Matters because:
The launch shows strong demand for new blockchain infrastructure, even if activity is currently meme-coin dominated.

Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir

tweet.text:
Three years ago, we launched Theory Ventures with a simple premise : AI would reshape how software is built, sold, deployed, & operated. Within that world, we would build a concentrated, thesis-driven firm.

The market moved faster than even the most bullish expectations after the ChatGPT moment. Frontier models leapt from delicate demos to production systems. Open source models have become substitutes for enterprise workloads. Inference emerged as the dominant market in AI.

Underpinning all of this, AI compresses time. New models are released every 41 days. Companies reach $100m in revenue in record time. We all achieve more faster.

In celebration of our anniversary, we wanted to trace that mechanism through the market shifts of the last three years.

The first casualty of compressed time is the old language of venture capital. Seed, Series A, Series B categories still exist, but they describe the financial product companies seek rather than rather than company maturity. Venture fir

A review of patterns we've observed in physical AI over the last 2-3 years, as the field has continued to make technical progress and aggregate talent and capital across new and existing companies: 

In 2023-2024, we started noticing more teams building for a given layer of the robotics stack. Whereas historically teams built all their own infrastructure, the promise of scaling laws for robotics meant generality was on the table, and what generality enables is for teams to build on top of a common platform — a huge advantage given the long tail nature of the physical world. We also posited that given the variance of the physical world, the proliferation of such a platform might resemble more of an open ecosystem in the vein of Android.  

This dynamic could kick off an apps>infrastructure cycle that robotics has historically lacked, but every other major technology cycle has seen (web, mobile, crypto, etc.). This is partially because there has been no breakout robotics app, but maybe,

Regulation.text:
Daily news snapshot: Altcoins watchlist
1. Arbitrum (ARB): +20%
• Market cap: ≈$3.5 billion
• ARB surged about 20% on July 10 after Robinhood Chain launched using Arbitrum as its underlying technology provider.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain will allocate 10% of its protocol net revenue back to the Arbitrum ecosystem.
• The move highlights ecosystem expansion and revenue-sharing mechanics.
• Current activity remains primarily meme-coin driven.
Matters because:
The Robinhood Chain tie-in gives Arbitrum a clear ecosystem catalyst, though the current usage mix is still largely speculative.
2. Cash Cat (CASHCAT): +40% intraday
• Market cap: $180 million
• The meme coin briefly hit an all-time high market cap of $180 million.
Catalysts:
• Robinhood Chain's explosive first week with $1 billion+ in DEX volume.
Matters because:
It shows how quickly speculative meme-coin activity can accelerate on a new chain, but the move lacks fundamental backing.
3. Solana (SOL): ETF inflow of $150,500
• Market cap: ≈$70 billion
• Solana spot ETF (TSOL) recorded a net inflow of $150,500 on July 10, with total SOL spot ETF assets at $901 million.
Matters because:
Even a modest inflow points to continued institutional interest in Solana despite broader caution.

tweet.text:
Morgan Stanley: Humanoids

Shifts in BoM Cost Composition (2026e–2040e)

> Rising Chips/Compute Cost: The share of BoM cost for Chips is projected to triple, rising sharply from 5% in 2026e to 15% by 2040e.

> Sensors (Force/Tactile): Remain the largest single component cost initially at 24% in 2026e, but steadily decline to 17% by 2040e.

> Motors: Hold a relatively stable but slightly declining share, moving from 22% down to 19%.

> Reducers: Drop slightly from 15% to 12%.

Projected Demand & Market Growth

> Motors: Projected to reach a massive US$2,073bn in market size, representing a 170x increase.

> Reducers: Expected to reach US$1,370bn (a 157x increase).

> Edge Compute: Reaches US$1,233bn, driven by the single highest multiplier growth of 1,904x.

> Bearings: Set for a staggering 370x growth to a value of US$316bn.

> Cameras & LiDAR: Cameras scale by 84x (US$268bn), while LiDAR scales by 40x (US$128bn).

Robotics.text:
The Decade Ahead
> The next decade will be shaped by three structural forces: AI-driven automation, a shift to a multipolar world, and demographic change, with intelligence becoming the world's most valuable resource.
> With agents, robotics, and edge AI driving a massive productivity boom.
Source:
https://members.delphidigital.io/reports/the-decade-ahead?utm_source=twitter&utm_medium=social#automation-multi-polarity-demographics-d836

An Investor’s Handbook I - AI Optics
Driven by massive AI computing demands, optical connections are progressively replacing copper from long-distance links down to chip-level architectures because photons offer vastly superior bandwidth with lower energy loss and heat
While copper still holds a cost and latency edge for short-range connections inside the server rack, the broader optical transition hinges on integrating mature silicon chips with specialized indium phosphide light sources and advanced packaging
https://nuttycld.substack.com/p/an-investors-handbook-i-ai-optics

An Investor's Handbook II - AI Power (1)
AI datacenter expansion faces a major timeline mismatch between rapid GPU releases (~18 months) and lengthy power grid interconnection approvals (~61 months)
As a result, the market value has shifted from electricity generation capacity to companies that can optimize energy efficiency, reduce loss, or accelerate delivery timelines across the power path
https://nuttycld.substack.com/p/an-investors-handbook-ii-ai-power

SEC proposes "Regulation Crypto" framework for easier capital raising –
Link
The SEC is preparing a new crypto regulation proposal as early as July 2026. It would allow startups to raise up to $5 million with simple white-paper disclosures and projects up to $75 million in any 12-month period.
Matters because:
It would mark a shift from enforcement-heavy regulation toward a more structured capital-raising path for crypto startups.

tweet.text:
SEC is preparing to propose a new crypto regulation as early as July 2026 that would make it significantly easier for startups to raise capital. 

Highlighted in a recent Yahoo Finance segment with analyst Scott Melker, the plan—referred to as Regulation Crypto—creates temporary exemptions from full securities registration under the 1933 Securities Act. 

It includes a startup pathway allowing projects to operate for up to four years with simple white-paper disclosures and raise up to $5 million, a fundraising exemption permitting up to $75 million in any 12-month period with basic disclosures, and a safe harbor for decentralizing projects that covers DeFi and tokenized securities. 

The goal, according to SEC Chair Paul Atkins, is to provide regulatory clarity, reduce enforcement risks, attract activity onshore, and position the United States as the global leader in crypto while still protecting investors.

This marks a clear shift from the prior enforcement-focused approach to one th

Brave releases BAT roadmap 4.0 with unified wallet and stablecoin payment protocol –
Link
Brave launched BAT roadmap 4.0 on July 9, adding a unified Brave Wallet and a new stablecoin payment protocol called BravePay.
• Unified wallet with traditional payments + self-custodied crypto
• BravePay, a privacy-focused stablecoin payment protocol
• Planned Brave Rewards payment cards
• Creator contribution protocol for AI content usage
Matters because:
The roadmap expands BAT’s ecosystem beyond basic rewards and could support wider adoption.
